title: first-match dead-dispatch sweep — 24 names × ~26 sites × 252 LOC removed
summary: Apply ω-stdlib-14's first-match-wins audit to the rest of fn call_builtin (L9927). Enumerated 24 builtin names with 2+ dispatch sites inside the function (write_file had 3); proved the later sites unreachable by inheritance from stdlib-8/14's flat-chain analysis (no conditional gate or scope re-entry between sites). Removed 252 LOC across 4 contiguous bands (Band A: chr/to_json/from_json @13633-13654; Band B: read_file/read_lines/write_file/eprintln/eprint/exit @13727-13781; Band C2: 12-block I/O+error sweep @14417-14530; Band C1: char_code/from_char_code @14274-14296; Band C3: hex/bin @14573-14609). 30 LOC of marker comments added (~1 per band, indexing the canonical sites). Net: -222 LOC. Several dead blocks were *divergent* from canonical (e.g. `error` used old val_map model vs canonical TAG_ERROR; `hex` had "0x" prefix vs canonical un-prefixed) — divergences are documented in the closure for forensic reference, but had zero behavioural impact since they never executed.

predecessors.omega_stdlib_8: len/to_string/type_of/int|to_int|to_i64/float|to_float/bool sweep — established marker-comment pattern at L13609-13611
predecessors.omega_stdlib_14: parse_int/parse_float — single-name proof of the same pattern, line-aligned with band A
predecessors.omega_stdlib_4: single-quote-escape fix on dir_exists/mkdir/list_dir — applied defensively to BOTH live and dead sites; this sweep removes the defensive dead-mirror

context.audit_method: awk over self/hexa_full.hexa: capture (fn, name, line) tuples for every `if name == "X"` inside fn call_builtin; emit pairs where same name appears 2+ times. Yielded 24 unique names + write_file's 3-way. Each pair confirmed dead by inheritance (flat dispatch chain).
context.problem: Multiple bands of duplicate dispatch had accumulated over previous self-host iterations. Each duplicate carried a small drift cost (subsequent edits sometimes patched only one site, sometimes both, leaving silent divergence). Earlier ω-stdlib-* cycles addressed this incrementally; this cycle finishes the sweep.

files_edited.marker_comments_added: 5 (one per band) — each indexes the canonical L for the deduped names + brief note on any divergence the dead block carried

divergence_inventory_dead_vs_canonical.purpose: forensic — divergences had zero observable impact (dead blocks never ran), but documented for future reference if any future cycle resurrects similar code

items.name: read_file
items.divergence: dead lacked is_error handling; canonical (L13313) returns TAG_ERROR Val on host error path

items.name: write_file
items.divergence: dead lacked try/catch; canonical (L13326) wraps in try

items.name: error
items.divergence: dead used val_map({message, trace}) old model; canonical (L13546) returns TAG_ERROR Val with str_val=msg

items.name: is_error
items.divergence: dead probed for 'message' key in TAG_MAP; canonical (L13539) checks tag == TAG_ERROR

items.name: hex
items.divergence: dead added '0x' prefix and handled negatives explicitly; canonical (L13508) delegates to host hex() which returns un-prefixed digits ('ff' not '0xff', 'fffffffffffffff1' for -15)

items.name: bin
items.divergence: dead added '0b' prefix; canonical (L13512) delegates to host bin() un-prefixed

items.name: dir_exists / mkdir / list_dir
items.divergence: no semantic divergence — both got the ω-stdlib-4 single-quote-escape fix as a defensive mirror

validation.result: exactly ONE site per name across all 24+2 deduped names

all 18 exercised builtins return expected values; the val_str_array undefined-fn error in the test was a fixture bug (val_str_array is not a builtin), unrelated to the deduped names — flow continued past it

scope_constraints_honoured: minimal blast — pure dead-code removal, no live behaviour change
no parallel-session collision — hexa-lang dirty list is bench/native_gate.c/hexa_v2/escalator.plist, NOT self/hexa_full.hexa
no commit in anima — closure JSON written as untracked
